core_mechanism: a minimal-norm worst-case (adversarial) input perturbation found by constrained optimization that maximally diverts one coupled output of a joint two-headed predictor while holding a second, normally-correlated output near its unperturbed value — deliberately decoupling two outputs that share a common latent representation
brief_reason: The abstract describes 'small visual perturbations' optimized to 'break the alignment' between a model's two coupled outputs (imagined future vs. executed action), with a variant that maximizes action shift while keeping the predicted future close to its clean value, i.e. a norm-constrained adversarial perturbation that decouples two coupled outputs.
